mappings.rationaleAI: Glass science covers glass as an amorphous solid material and its manufacturing processes, relevant to casting/molding nonmetals and property modification of materials.
The Iron Age covers iron smelting processes and the production of metal tools, relevant to mineral processing, extractive metallurgy, and physical metallurgy.
The Industrial Revolution covers iron and steel production processes, relevant to extractive metallurgy and physical metallurgy techniques.
Semiconductor processing including doping to create n-type and p-type materials relates to property modification of materials through chemical processing.
The electron's role in vacuum tubes and semiconductor manufacturing connects to property modification and materials processing in electronics.
